AddMetadataRequest.description: Request body for adding metadata (POST - merge)

AddRoleTagsRequest.description: Request body for adding role tags (POST - merge)

CreateVmRequest.description: Request body for creating a new VM

Disk.description: Disk attached to a VM (bhyve VMs)

Error.description: Error response from a Node.js Triton service

request_id.description: Request ID for tracing (optional, not always present)

onerror.description: Onerror chain (tasks to run on failure)

vm_uuid.description: VM UUID this job operates on

JobResponse.description: Response when VM creation or action returns a job

MetadataObject.description:
Key-value metadata (values can be strings, booleans, or numbers)

Newtype wrapper rather than a type alias so the generated OpenAPI spec carries `MetadataObject` as a named schema rather than an anonymous `additionalProperties` object. See the note on `Tags` below for the full rationale.

MigrationAction.description: Migration sub-action for the `migrate` VM action

MigrationPhase.description:
Migration phase

Represents the current phase of a migration operation.

primary.description: Whether this is the primary NIC

allow_ip_spoofing.description: Whether this NIC is allowed to have spoofed IP addresses

allow_mac_spoofing.description: Whether this NIC is allowed to have spoofed MAC addresses

allow_restricted_traffic.description: Whether this NIC allows restricted traffic

network_uuid.description: Network UUID to add NIC on

PutVmRequest.description: Request body for PUT /vms/:uuid (replace VM object)

PutVmsRequest.description: Request body for PUT /vms (bulk update VMs for a server)

SetMetadataRequest.description: Request body for setting metadata (PUT - replace all)

SetRoleTagsRequest.description: Request body for setting role tags (PUT - replace all)

role_tags.description: Role tags to set (replaces all existing)

StatusesResponse.description:
Response mapping VM UUIDs to their statuses.

Newtype wrapper rather than a type alias so the generated OpenAPI spec carries `StatusesResponse` as a named schema, giving downstream clients a named type for the VM-statuses endpoint's response rather than an anonymous map.

Tags.description:
Key-value tags (values can be strings, booleans, or numbers)

Newtype wrapper rather than a type alias so the generated OpenAPI spec carries `Tags` as a named schema rather than an anonymous `additionalProperties` object. A `pub type Tags = HashMap<String, Value>` is erased by schemars at compile time, causing every field typed `Tags` to inline the map shape and downstream code generators (Progenitor, oapi-codegen) to emit unnamed `serde_json::Map` / `map[string]interface{}` types per field. The newtype preserves the name so all clients see a single `Tags` type.

TaskChainEntry.description: Task chain entry in a job

TaskResult.description: Result of a task in the job

Vm.description:
VM object returned by VMAPI

This is a large object with many fields. All fields use snake_case.

free_space.description: Free space in bytes (bhyve with flexible disk, may be negative)

server_uuid.description: Server UUID (compute node hosting the VM)

VmBrand.description:
VM/Container brand as returned by VMAPI.

The brand determines the virtualization/containerization technology used. - `bhyve`: FreeBSD hypervisor for hardware VMs - `builder`: Internal brand for image build zones (not provisionable via CloudAPI) - `joyent`: Native SmartOS zone - `joyent-minimal`: Minimal SmartOS zone - `kvm`: KVM hardware VM - `lx`: Linux-branded zone (Linux containers)

oneOf.description: Internal brand for image build zones (not provisionable via CloudAPI)

VmState.description:
VM state

These states reflect the possible values returned by VMAPI.

VmStatus.description: Status entry for a single VM

info.description: Triton VMAPI - internal HTTP API for managing virtual machines in a Triton datacenter

put.description: Used by cn-agent to sync VM state for an entire compute node.
put.summary: Bulk update VMs for a server

post.description:
Dispatches an action on the VM. The action may be specified in the request body (`{"action": "start", ...}`) or as a query parameter (`?action=start`). Body takes precedence over the query parameter.

Available actions: start, stop, kill, reboot, reprovision, update, add_nics, update_nics, remove_nics, create_snapshot, rollback_snapshot, delete_snapshot, create_disk, resize_disk, delete_disk, migrate.

The request body varies by action type.

parameters.description: Action to perform. Optional in the query string because clients may send it in the request body instead.
parameters.name: action

parameters.description: If true, wait for job completion before returning (default: false)
parameters.name: sync

put.description: Replaces the entire VM object (used by cn-agent for sync).

get.description: Returns all customer metadata for a VM.

post.description: Adds/merges customer metadata with existing values.

put.description: Replaces all customer metadata with the provided values.

Returns all internal metadata for a VM.

put.description: Replaces all internal metadata with the provided values.

delete.description: Removes a specific internal metadata key.

get.description: Returns the value of a specific internal metadata key.
Returns jobs associated with a specific VM.
Returns process information for the VM from CNAPI.

post.description: Adds role tags to the VM.
